Details: One lab study observed >90% removal of perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA) via oxidation by persulfate in hot (85 degrees C) water after 30 hours reaction time. Removal in cooler water (25 degrees C) was negligible. The presence of iron reduced PFOA oxidation somewhat. Degradation intermediates included perfluoroheptanoic acid (PFHpA), perfluorohexanoic acid (PFHxA), perfluoropentanoic acid (PFPeA), and perfluorobutanoic acid (PFBA) [2550].
